title: She is running Boston in honor of her mother who died of brain cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Madison Oakes from Boxford is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in honor of her mother who died from brain cancer.
Oakes credits the MGB cancer center for extending her mother's life after she was diagnosed with brain cancer.
A nurse in oncology, Oakes seeks to support and inspire others battling cancer as she runs the marathon.

title: Boston police fatally shoot suspect allegedly wielding sword near Northeastern University campus
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police shot and killed a man allegedly wielding a sword near Northeastern University after he attacked officers and EMS personnel.
The suspect was reportedly experiencing a mental health crisis; an officer and two emergency responders sustained non-life-threatening injuries.
Areas around Hemenway Street were closed as police and the Suffolk County District Attorney's Office investigate the incident.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her godmother who died of brain cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Abigail Dubois, 23, from Hudson, NH, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor her late godmother.
Dubois is donating to the Cancer Care Equity program at MGH, inspired by her godmother's brain cancer treatment.
Dubois qualified for the Boston Marathon at the Lake Erie Marathon in September 2025.

title: 1 dead, 2 hospitalized in fatal Brockton house fire, officials say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A Brockton house fire on Saturday resulted in the death of a woman and two others hospitalized.
Fire ignited on Glendale Ave. engulfing the second floor; officials say the cause is undetermined but not suspicious.
The Office of the Medical Examiner will decide the cause of death; investigations are ongoing by local and state authorities.

title: NH suspect who allegedly shot at family members killed in officer-involved shooting
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Matthew Masse, 38, was shot and killed by police in a New Hampshire shootout on Saturday night.
Earlier, Masse allegedly wounded a Nottingham police officer and shot at family members, prompting a manhunt.
Residents near Ham Road and Nottingham Road were asked to shelter in place as police pursued Masse in the woods.

title: She’s running Boston with her best friend after both diagnosed with cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Meg Gannon and her friend Peggy, both breast cancer survivors, are running the Boston Marathon for Mass General.
Gannon, from Canton, Mass., postponed running in 2025 due to additional surgeries but will participate in 2026.
The duo is running to celebrate life, survivorship, and to honor those battling cancer, inspired by personal experiences.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Jack Black hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Jack Black hosted the April 4 episode of 'SNL', marking his fifth time hosting the show.
Musical guest Jack White joined Black, performing for the fifth time, including his past with The White Stripes.
The episode featured a cameo-filled five-timers club sketch and an SNL-ified version of 'Seven Nation Army'.

title: Jaylen Brown opens up about past criticisms of his left hand: ‘There was some truth to it’
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jaylen Brown addresses past criticisms about his left hand, acknowledging issues due to a 2021 wrist injury.
Brown’s improvement in using his left hand is attributed to a 2023 wrist surgery and subsequent recovery.
This season, Brown averages career-highs with 28.7 points, 7.0 rebounds, and 5.3 assists per game.

title: ‘The Red Sox were my dream job’: Don Orsillo reflects on NESN tenure, reunites with former colleagues
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Don Orsillo returns to Fenway Park with the San Diego Padres, reflecting on his tenure with the Boston Red Sox.
Orsillo was the play-by-play voice for the Red Sox from 2001 to 2015 before joining the Padres in 2016.
Current NESN staff, including Jim Rice and Tom Caron, reunited with Orsillo during his visit to Boston.

title: Alex Cora explains his decision to slot Roman Anthony at DH early this season
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Roman Anthony has been used frequently as DH due to defensive struggles, according to Red Sox manager Alex Cora.
Anthony contributed offensively against the Padres with a triple and a hit, despite a critical defensive error.
Red Sox are struggling overall, holding a 2-6 record before Sunday's game against the Padres at Fenway Park.
